heading: Critical area service agreements
label: 3324 Critical area service agreements
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a)(2)(A), was in the original a reference to “section 1322(b)”, meaning , which has been translated as reading to reflect the probable intent of Congress because section 1322 does not have a subsec. (b) and section 1323(b) relates to designation of critical areas by tribes.Section 3323(b) of this titlesection 1322(b) of Pub. L. 102–325section 1323(b) of Pub. L. 102–325

children.text: to undertake a course of study at an eligible institution (as that term is defined in ) in an area of critical need, as determined under , and to pursue that course of study to its completion; andsection 1085(a) of title 20section 3323 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to perform, for each academic year for which the student receives federally funded higher education assistance under a critical area service agreement, one calendar year of service to the tribe or organization in an occupation that is in a critical area designated by the tribe pursuant to section 3323(b) of this title, commencing not later than 6 months after the student ceases to carry at an institution of higher education at least one-half the normal full-time academic workload as determined by the institution; or1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: to repay such assistance to the Secretary, together with interest thereon at a rate prescribed by the Secretary by regulation, in monthly or quarterly installments over not more than 5 years.
shall be provided compensation, benefits, and working conditions at the same level and to the same extent as any other employee working a similar length of time and doing the same type of work;

may be treated as providing services to the tribe or organization if the student provides services for members of the tribe or organization that are approved by the tribe or organization and agreed to by the student even though such services are performed while the student is employed by a Federal, State, or local agency or instrumentality or by a nonprofit or for-profit private institution or organization; and
may obtain the benefits of a waiver or suspension in accordance with the requirements of subsection (c).

children.heading: Waiver and suspension of service agreement
children.label: c Waiver and suspension of service agreement

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 1 Waiver
children.text: An Indian tribe or tribal organization may, by formal action, waive the service agreement of an Indian student for just cause, as determined in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary. The tribe or organization shall notify the Secretary in writing of any waiver granted under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be suspended for not more than 18 months if, at the request of the student, the tribe or organization determines that there are no employment opportunities available in any critical service area; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be suspended if the student ceases to attend an institution of higher education as a consequence of an institutional determination of unsatisfactory performance.

children.heading: Pro rata reduction for partial services
children.label: d Pro rata reduction for partial services
children.text: The Secretary shall, by regulation, provide for the pro rata reduction of repayment obligations under subsection (a)(2) in the case of any student who partially completes the service obligation of that student under subsection (a)(2)(A).

shall establish procedures for monitoring and evaluating the provisions of this subchapter, and provide a copy of such procedures to the Secretary and to each individual providing services under a critical area service agreement;
shall annually certify to the Secretary the identities of the individuals performing service under such agreements; and
shall annually certify to the Secretary the amount of service performed, and the amount remaining to be performed, by each such individual under such agreements.
